articles.title: Cotton Corrects Lower on Thursday
articles.expand: Cotton futures saw weakness on Thursday with contracts down 175 to 287 points in the front months. The US dollar index was $0.352 higher at $98.775. Crude oil was up $1 at $102.02. A meeting between President Trump and China’s President Xi in Beijing concluded early this morning. Few details...

articles.title: EURUSD sellers are leaning aganst corrective resistance targets keeping sellers in control
articles.expand: The EURUSD is trading sharply lower after breaking below a major technical support cluster defined by the 200-day moving average and the 38.2% retracement of the rally from the February 12 low. Both levels converged near 1.16806, creating an important barometer for buyers and sellers. Once the pair moved below that zone — and more importantly staye...

articles.title: Silver is sharply lower on higher USD/higher yields. Technically breaks the 100 day MA.
articles.expand: The price of silver is under intense pressure today, tumbling $6.55 or -7.84% to $76.90. The sharp decline marks the largest one-day percentage drop since March 3, when the metal fell -8.15%, and reflects a dramatic shift in momentum after silver once again failed near a major resistance target around the $90 level. articles.title: Euro: Growth downgrades and softer outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists highlights that Eurozone 2026 GDP forecasts have been cut more than United States (US) projections in percentage terms, reinforcing United States (US) outperformance.

articles.title: Euro falls as hawkish Fed expectations boost the US Dollar, Treasury yields
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) extends losses against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday, with EUR/USD slipping to near one-month lows as hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) expectations boost the Greenback and US Treasury yields.

articles.title: Gold Weekly Forecast: Sellers return on hawkish Fed bets and Middle East uncertainty
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) edged higher at the beginning of the week but made a sharp U-turn as the US Dollar (USD) gathered strength on hot inflation data, closing the week in the red by losing more than 3% of its value.

articles.title: Dollar Soars and Gold Plunges as Global Bond Yields Climb
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rallied to a 2.5-week high today and is up by +0.39%. The dollar is climbing today on concerns that strong US economic news and soaring crude oil prices will prompt the Fed to tighten monetary policy, a bullish factor for the dollar. articles.title: Australian Dollar extends sharp decline as Fed hike repricing lifts US Dollar
articles.expand: AUD/USD falls sharply on Friday and trades around 0.7155 at the time of writing, down 0.91% on the day, after hitting its lowest level in more than a week. The pair remains under heavy selling pressure for the second consecutive day amid broad-based US Dollar (USD) strength.

articles.title: Japan: Solid GDP but JPY still weighed by trade – DBS
articles.expand: DBS economists Taimur Baig and Radhika Rao expect Japan’s 1Q Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to grow 1.8% QoQ saar, supported by firm exports and AI- and semiconductor-related investment, keeping their 0.5% full-year GDP forecast on track.

articles.title: Canada: Energy lifts CPI, BoC focus on core – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Senior Canada Economist Robert Both expects Canadian Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation to rise to 3.1% year-on-year in April, driven mainly by higher energy and food prices and base effects from last year’s carbon tax changes.

articles.title: US Dollar: Hawkish Fed repricing drives breakout – ING
articles.expand: ING's Francesco Pesole notes the US Dollar (USD) is gaining strong short-term momentum as hotter US data reinforce expectations for a Federal Reserve (Fed) hike.

articles.title: Gold drops to over one-week lows as hawkish Fed bets boost US Dollar, Treasury yields
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) slides to over one-week lows on Friday, pressured by a stronger US Dollar (USD) and rising Treasury yields as mounting Oil-driven inflation risks strengthen expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) could keep interest rates elevated for longer.

articles.title: Oil: Supply-chain stress scenarios – Rabobank

articles.title: Gold Falls on US Inflation Concerns as Week Ends in Losses
articles.expand: By Analytical Department RoboForex Gold continued its decline on Friday, falling to 4,619 USD per ounce. The week is set to close with losses of around 1%, as mounting US inflation puts pressure on the market. Rising prices reinforce expectations that the Federal Reserve may maintain elevated rates for longer or even resume rate hikes. […]

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Minor hurdle comes at 0.7280
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, prompting AUD/USD to confront the area of multi-year peaks. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated domestic inflation and the RBA’s cautious approach.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.
